---
title: "Audit and Review Results"
description: "Individual review results for the working-paper package."
---

The working paper was reviewed through several focused audits. Each review
tested a different failure mode: historical overreach, rhetorical
overinterpretation, theological attribution, source and citation integrity,
counterevidence, anachronism, evidence coding, and publication readiness.
The pages linked below pair a short public-facing purpose statement with the
underlying review artifact, so readers can see both why the review exists and
what it concluded.

## Review Pages

| Review | What It Checks | Result |
|---|---|---|
| [Historical review](historical-review.html) | Whether the paper overgeneralizes the Civil War, ignores soldier agency, or overstates bodily omission. | Several critiques partially accepted; historical limits remain disclosed. |
| [Rhetorical review](rhetorical-review.html) | Whether ceremonial convention, sacrifice language, and Everett comparison are handled carefully. | Several cautions accepted or partially accepted; sacrifice claim retained with limits. |
| [Theological-language review](theological-language-review.html) | Whether public sacred rhetoric is mistaken for Lincoln's private belief. | Distinctions strengthened; Second Inaugural and Christianization cautions retained. |
| [Source and citation review](source-and-citation-review.html) | Whether citations, quotation status, and source limits are adequate. | No critical citation failures; quotation-finality caveats remain. |
| [Critical countervoice review](critical-countervoice-review.html) | Whether counter-readings from ceremony, Democratic criticism, and later memory weaken the argument. | Countervoices narrowed the argument rather than defeating the working-paper claim. |
| [Anachronism review](anachronism-review.html) | Whether later theoretical vocabulary is imported into the 1863 material. | Some vocabulary narrowed; remaining anachronism limits disclosed. |
| [Disconfirmation review](disconfirmation-review.html) | Whether serious falsifiers and competing interpretations were tested. | Six falsifiers and six competing interpretations were preserved for review. |
| [Coding review](coding-review.html) | Whether evidence coding is structurally adequate and preserves uncertainty. | Evidence coding supports working analysis with noted risks. |
| [Draft source audit](draft-source-audit.html) | Whether draft sources, sections, citations, quotations, and rendering were ready. | No critical failures, with recommended next checks. |
| [Publication approval review](publication-approval-review.html) | Whether the final package was ready for public release. | Approved only as a transparent public working-paper package. |

## Reading Order

For publication readiness, start with the
[publication approval review](publication-approval-review.html), then read the
[source and citation review](source-and-citation-review.html) and
[critical countervoice review](critical-countervoice-review.html).

For interpretive scope, start with the
[historical review](historical-review.html), [rhetorical review](rhetorical-review.html),
[theological-language review](theological-language-review.html), and
[anachronism review](anachronism-review.html).
